01,"How to Make New Friends","B1","3","30-40 分鐘","用英文自然地自我介紹;詢問別人的興趣和生活;使用追問句延續對話;完成一段一分鐘的英文口說","先不要查單字，試著用英文回答（打字或用說的都可以）！
Is it easy or difficult for you to make new friends?
column2: 可以用 It is easy / difficult for me because... 開頭

column1: Where do you usually meet new people?
column2: 例如 at school, at work, online...

column1: What do you usually say when you meet someone for the first time?
column2: 例如 Hi, my name is...","看到標題 ""How to Make New Friends""，你猜這篇文章會談什麼？

column1: Meeting new people can feel exciting, but it can also feel uncomfortable. You may not know what to say, or you may worry about making a mistake. The good news is that making friends does not require perfect English or a perfect personality. It usually starts with one small conversation.

First, begin with a simple greeting. You can say, ""Hi, I'm Alex. What's your name?"" After introducing yourself, ask an easy question about the situation. Questions like these are natural because they are connected to the place or event.

Next, listen carefully to the other person's answer. Good conversations are not only about speaking. If someone says, ""I like watching movies,"" do not stop at ""Me too."" Ask a follow-up question such as, ""What kind of movies do you like?""

Finally, do not put too much pressure on yourself. Not every conversation will become a friendship, and that is okay. Be friendly, stay curious and give the conversation time.","Q: What is the main idea of the article?
A: You need perfect English to make friends.
B: Simple questions and careful listening can help you make friends.
C: You should only talk about movies with new people.
ANS: 1
Q: Why should you ask questions about the situation?
A: Because they feel natural and are easy to answer.
B: Because they test the other person's English.
C: Because they end the conversation quickly.
ANS: 0
Q: True or False? A good conversation is only about speaking.
A: True
B: False
ANS: 1
EXPLAIN: A good conversation includes both speaking and listening.
Q: What should you do after someone tells you about an interest?
A: Say ""Me too"" and stop.
B: Change the topic.
C: Show interest and ask a follow-up question.
ANS: 2","uncomfortable
column5: I felt uncomfortable when nobody spoke.

column1: PATTERN: How long + have / has + subject + past participle...?
EXPLAIN: 這個句型用來詢問某件事從過去開始，到現在持續了多久。回答時：for + 一段時間、since + 起始時間。
EXAMPLES: How long have you lived here?;How long have you studied English?;She has worked there for six months.
PRACTICE: I have lived here ______ five years.
PRACTICE: She has known her best friend ______ high school.
PRACTICE: Choose the correct sentence.
column3: How long do you studied English?;How long have you studied English?;How long are you study English?

column1: TALK: Your name;Where you are from;What you do;Your hobbies;What you usually do on weekends
STARTERS: Hi, I'm...;I'm from...;In my free time, I enjoy...;I'm really into...;On weekends, I usually...
TARGETS: i'm from;i enjoy;i'm really into;on weekends;how about you

SAMPLE: Hi, I'm Kevin. I'm from Taiwan, and I currently work in marketing. In my free time, I enjoy watching movies and playing basketball.","SITUATION: 你第一次參加英文聊天活動，遇到另一位參加者 Leo。目標：自我介紹、問對方為什麼來、問興趣、用追問句、禮貌結束對話。
NPC: Leo
TURN: Hi! I haven't seen you before. I'm Leo.
column3: Hi, I'm {name}. Nice to meet you!:::自我介紹＋問候:::2;Hello.:::只打招呼:::0

column1: TURN: Nice to meet you too! Is this your first time here?
column3: Yes, it is! What brings you here?:::回答＋反問:::2;Yes, first time.:::只回答:::0

column1: TURN: My friend invited me. How about you?
column3: I'm taking an English class here.:::分享自己的原因:::2;I don't know.:::有點冷場:::0
